title: Fix category filtering (All, Girls Only, Boys Only, Co-Living) on Home page

requirements.summary: Fix the category filter functionality on the Home page so that selecting 'All Hostels', 'Girls Only', 'Boys Only', or 'Co-Living' correctly filters the displayed hostel listings
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting 'All Hostels' displays every hostel in the listings grid
Selecting 'Girls Only' displays only hostels with category 'Girls'
Selecting 'Boys Only' displays only hostels with category 'Boys'
Selecting 'Co-Living' displays only hostels with category 'Co-Living'
The active/selected category tab is visually highlighted
Switching between category tabs updates the listings grid immediately without a page reload
If no hostels match the selected category, the empty-state UI is shown

file_operations.description: Fix the category filtering logic to correctly switch between useGetAllHostels (for 'All Hostels') and useGetHostelsByCategory (for specific categories). Ensure the selectedCategory state is properly passed to the query hooks and that the filtering logic correctly applies the category filter to the hostel list. Verify that the CategoryFilter component's onCategoryChange callback correctly updates the selectedCategory state and triggers a re-fetch of hostels matching the selected category. Ensure the empty-state UI is displayed when no hostels match the selected category.

Verify and fix the CategoryFilter component state management to ensure the selected category is correctly tracked and visually highlighted. Ensure the onCategoryChange callback is invoked with the correct category value ('All', 'Girls', 'Boys', or 'Co-Living') when a user clicks a category button. Confirm that the active category button receives the correct gradient styling to indicate selection.

Review the useGetHostelsByCategory and useGetAllHostels hooks to ensure they are correctly enabled based on the selectedCategory state and that they fetch the appropriate data from the backend. Verify that useGetHostelsByCategory passes the correct category string to the backend actor's getHostelsByCategory method and that useGetAllHostels calls listHostels. Ensure query keys are unique per category to prevent cache conflicts.
